title: Distinguishing yourself = bringing value to the table
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line manifesto-style note from May 2020: "Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead. Value proposition." — Yahir's earliest crisp articulation of value proposition as distinction.
  • Captured at age 17, mid-COVID pandemic era, when the "how do I matter" question had no school or sport to absorb it.
  • Anti-zero-sum frame: distinction is not being above others, it's adding what isn't on the table — pre-figures the nonobvious and asking the right question dispositions.
  • Tagged in the corpus as a manifesto style note: zero context, zero todos, just an axiom — the genre Yahir keeps returning to.
  • Direct lineage to the no runway discipline of the 2025-2026 rankett and brotein eras: every action has to add value, no fluff.

Source quote

"Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead.
Value proposition"

Open questions

  • What triggered the line in early May 2020? Quarantine boredom plus a business book? The phrase "value proposition" suggests he'd just read or watched something formal.
  • Is this the earliest value-proposition invocation in the vault, or does an earlier raw note pre-date it?

Wikilinks

value proposition as distinction · nonobvious · asking the right question · no runway discipline · covid pandemic era · rankett · brotein · be your own original · disconnected from outcome
